name: rattail-tempmon

metadata.name: rattail-tempmon
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Retail Software Framework - Temperature monitoring add-on

documentation:
# rattail-tempmon

Rattail is a retail software framework, released under the GNU General Public
License.

This is the `rattail-tempmon` package, which provides a database schema, and
client/server daemons for recording and processing temperature data.

Please see Rattail's [home page] for more information.

[home page]: https://rattailproject.org/

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.TempmonBase
documentation: Generic base class for all tempmon-related emails; adds common sample data.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_critical_high_temp
documentation: Sent when a tempmon probe takes a "critical high" temperature reading.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_critical_low_temp
documentation: Sent when a tempmon probe takes a "critical low" temperature reading.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_error
documentation: Sent when a tempmon probe is noticed to have some error, i.e. no current readings.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_client_offline
documentation: Sent when a tempmon client has been marked offline.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_good_temp
documentation:
Sent whenever a tempmon probe first takes a "good temp" reading, after
having previously had some bad reading(s).

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_high_temp
documentation:
Sent when a tempmon probe takes a reading which is above the "maximum good
temp" range, but still below the "critically high temp" threshold.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_low_temp
documentation:
Sent when a tempmon probe takes a reading which is below the "minimum good
temp" range, but still above the "critically low temp" threshold.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.emails.tempmon_disabled_probes
documentation: Notifies of any Tempmon client devices or probes which are disabled.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.settings.rattail_tempmon_probe_default_critical_max_timeout
documentation:
Default value to be used as Critical High Timeout value, for any probe
which does not have this timeout defined.
TempMon

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.settings.rattail_tempmon_probe_default_critical_min_timeout
documentation:
Default value to be used as Critical Low Timeout value, for any probe which
does not have this timeout defined.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.settings.rattail_tempmon_probe_default_error_timeout
documentation:
Default value to be used as Error Timeout value, for any probe which does
not have this timeout defined.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.settings.rattail_tempmon_probe_default_good_max_timeout
documentation:
Default value to be used as High Timeout value, for any probe which does
not have this timeout defined.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.settings.rattail_tempmon_probe_default_good_min_timeout
documentation:
Default value to be used as Low Timeout value, for any probe which does not
have this timeout defined.

modules.name: rattail_tempmon.db.alembic.env
documentation: Alembic Environment Script
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: rattail_tempmon.db.alembic.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.

functions.name: rattail_tempmon.db.alembic.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.config.TempmonConfigExtension
documentation:
Config extension for tempmon; adds tempmon DB engine/Session etc.  Expects
something like this in your config:

```ini
[rattail_tempmon.db]
default.url = postgresql://localhost/tempmon
```

Config object will get two new attributes:

> - `tempmon_engines`
> - `tempmon_engine`

Additionally, {class}`Session` will be configured with the default engine.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Appliance
documentation: Represents an appliance which is monitored by tempmon.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Client
documentation: Represents a tempmon client.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Probe

methods.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Probe.last_reading
documentation: Returns the reading which was taken most recently for this probe.

methods.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Probe.start_status
documentation:
Update the "started" timestamp field for the given status.  This is
used to track e.g. when we cross the "high temp" threshold, as a
separate event from when the "critical high temp" threshold is reached.

Note that in addition to setting the appropriate timestamp field, this
also will clear out other timestamp fields, according to the specific
(new) status.

methods.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Probe.status_started
documentation: Return the timestamp indicating when the given status started.

methods.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Probe.timeout_for_status
documentation:
Returns the timeout value for the given status.  This is be the number
of minutes by which we should delay the initial email for the status.
Represents a probe connected to a tempmon client.

classes.name: rattail_tempmon.db.model.Reading
documentation: Represents a single temperature reading from a tempmon probe.
